title: Lattice Packing of Spheres in High Dimensions Using a Stochastically Evolving Ellipsoid
speaker: Boaz Klartag
abstract: We prove that in any dimension n there exists an origin-symmetric ellipsoid of volume c n^2 that contains no points of Z^n other than the origin. Here c greater than 0 is a universal constant. Equivalently, there exists a lattice sphere packing in R^n whose density is at least c n^2 / 2^n. Previously known constructions of sphere packings in R^n had densities of the order of magnitude of n / 2^n, up to logarithmic factors. Our proof utilizes a stochastically evolving ellipsoid that accumulates at least  c n^2 lattice points on its boundary, while containing no lattice points in its interior except for the origin.

proof_stages.label: Density, lattices, and ellipsoid normalization
proof_stages.summary: The speaker defines sphere-packing density and lattice packings, fixes the notations for lattice and unrestricted densities, and rewrites the scaled lattice density as the maximal volume of an origin-centered integer-free ellipsoid.

proof_stages.label: Known landscape and main lower bound
proof_stages.summary: Known exact dimensions, historical lower and upper bounds, physics heuristics, and Venkatesh's conjectural polynomial growth are reviewed before the new theorem is stated: one can find an integer-free ellipsoid of volume at least on the order of n squared.

proof_stages.label: Random unimodular lattices and Siegel summation
proof_stages.summary: The space of covolume-one lattices is equipped with its finite invariant measure, Siegel's mean-value formula is explained, and the first-moment argument recovers the Minkowski lower bound.

proof_stages.label: Rogers' Poisson heuristic and successive minima
proof_stages.summary: Random-lattice statistics are compared with a Poisson process, Rogers' argument using the first n shortest vectors and Minkowski's second theorem is sketched, and its limitation motivates using about n squared constraints instead of only n.

proof_stages.label: Constrained stochastic ellipsoid deformation
proof_stages.summary: The proposed construction deforms an integer-free ellipsoid while pinning every newly encountered lattice point to the boundary; a Poisson-process volume heuristic predicts n squared growth, and the rigorous process is defined as projected Brownian motion in the space of symmetric matrices.

proof_stages.label: Martingale determinant drift and contact estimates
proof_stages.summary: The process is stopped at a chosen short time, controlled via martingale quadratic variation and Brownian comparison, and the concavity of log determinant gives volume growth except for a penalty that is bounded through absorption probabilities for lattice points.

proof_stages.label: Siegel averaging finish and sharpness discussion
proof_stages.summary: Averaging the resulting lattice-point sum over random lattices with Siegel's formula converts it to an integral and yields determinant at most about n to the minus four, hence ellipsoid volume about n squared; the closing discussion addresses possible logarithmic gains, numerical simulation, and heuristic sharpness.
